summary: A member header may publish `[requirement.<name>]` tables — same namespace as the assembly roster, joined by `satisfies`, cross-publisher name collisions are admissibility findings

per.section: Decision: a requirement's publisher is any authored surface document

edit.description: Parse `[requirement.<name>]` clause modules on a member header — the same shape the assembly roster carries (`means`, `kind`, `package`, `required`), unknown-key-rejecting (specs/10-contracts.md, "unknown keys are rejected"). Surface them as a parsed collection beside the existing `Satisfies` modules (a `requirements(header)` reader mirroring `satisfies(header)`, plus an `add_requirement` emitter), and round-trip them on emit.

Carry a member's published requirements into its `Features` (or a parallel per-member collection) so main can gather them across every kind's members, mirroring how `satisfies` is already carried on `Features`.

Inside the layered (`if let Some(layer)`) path, union member-published requirements with `layer.requirements()` into the single `BTreeMap<String, Requirement>` fed to `coverage::check` and `roster::{admissibility,check,conformance}` — reusing `compose::Requirement` (its fields are pub). A name published by two surfaces (assembly ⊕ member, or two members) is one namespace: emit an admissibility finding on collision, never a shadow. `satisfies` fill and the existing coverage gate join them unchanged.

Add coverage: a member-published requirement is filled by another member's `satisfies` (green); an unfilled `required` member-published requirement is an unfilled finding; a name declared by two publishers is an admissibility collision finding.

tests.asserts: a `[requirement.<name>]` published in a member header joins the roster's coverage gate (filled by a `satisfies` on another member); an unfilled required one fires; a cross-publisher name collision is an admissibility finding.

acceptance: a member document's `[requirement.<name>]` tables publish into the one requirement namespace, are gated by the existing coverage check, and collide with other publishers as admissibility findings — the engine support the classed-corpus migration needs (an intent spec declaring the entities an architecture spec must satisfy); cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Engine consequence of law 8 + the classed-corpus Decision (specs/90-spec-system.md). Serialized behind REDD-CUSTOM-KINDS (both edit src/main.rs). Scoped to the layered coverage path; a member-published requirement in a temper.toml-less floor-only harness is out of scope — surfaced, not filed. The specs/ class placement reshuffle + header authoring is a HUMAN ceremony after this ships, never a build tick.

summary: Add the `agent` built-in harness kind — `.claude/agents/*.md` imported as one AGENT.md member document, checked by an authored agent package

gate.reason: more built-in kinds is the wrong direction under the requirements/satisfies reframe; the surface-language chain it waited on has shipped, so the blocker is priority alone — revive only if a story demands the agent kind

new.description: The Agent IR — the typed model for a Claude Code subagent at `.claude/agents/<name>.md`, mirroring `src/rule.rs`: typed YAML frontmatter on the projection side (`name`, `description`, optional `tools`/`model`), unknown keys preserved, content-faithful body, provenance. Identity is the path (file stem). The surface member is one AGENT.md document (clause-module header) like every kind.

The agent kind's authored built-in package (clauses + guidance) in the one-document medium, embedded with the std-lib by build.rs (the packages/ product tree — NOT a contracts/agent.toml). Clauses: `name` required + `allowed_chars` `[a-z0-9-]`; `description` required; `forbidden_keys` for the Cursor `.mdc` keys Claude Code ignores; an advisory `max_lines`.
Fixture-driven proof mirroring `tests/rule_contract.rs`: one broken agent per clause proving it fires, a clean agent that trips nothing, and an insta import round-trip proving idempotence.

edit.description: Add `pub mod agent;` beside the other artifact-kind modules (this entry owns the line).
Extend `Workspace` with `agents` loaded under `<dir>/agents/*` and a `WorkspaceError` arm; update the load tests.
Discover `.claude/agents/*.md`, project each to one `<into>/agents/<name>/AGENT.md` member document, add an `[[agent]]` lock row per agent.
Bind the agent package, wire admissibility + conformance streams and `by_kind` for the kind.

tests.asserts: each decidable clause of the agent package fires on its broken fixture and stays silent on a clean agent; import of an unchanged agent is idempotent (insta snapshot).

acceptance: `temper check` dispatches `.claude/agents/*.md` to the agent package (conformance) and that package passes admissibility; `temper import` projects agents as one-document members idempotently with `[[agent]]` lock rows; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Deferred — more built-in kinds is the wrong direction under the requirements/satisfies reframe; revive only if a story demands the agent kind. Shaped to surface-language forms: one AGENT.md member document + an authored packages/agent/PACKAGE.md (no contracts/agent.toml). On revival, serialize its shared-file blast radius (lib/check/extract/import/main) via blockedBy against any then-open entry touching those files.

summary: Ship acquisition channels — npm optionalDependencies binary wrapper, Homebrew, cargo install — auto-detected, fail-loud on a missing platform binary

per.section: Decision: acquisition rides the ecosystem's package managers

gate.reason: needs human release setup (npm org, Homebrew tap, signing/publish creds) before the mechanical wrappers can be validated end-to-end

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages, GitHub release assets, and the Homebrew tap; channel auto-detected.

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`, confirmed on disk). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages (Biome's pattern) plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, `specs/50-distribution.md`). Reconcile with the flume manifest (a non-private publish entry, or a split package dir) rather than clobbering the harness manifest. Scoped/prefixed names per the resolved (project-name) fork.

acceptance: A stranger can acquire the prebuilt binary via npm (platform-specific optionalDependencies), Homebrew, standalone release assets, or `cargo install`; a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip.
notes: Release infra, mostly outside `src/`; the cargo gates can't validate npm/Homebrew publish, and channels need human creds (npm org, tap, signing). NB: root `package.json` already exists as the *private* flume manifest — reconcile or split, never blindly create a second. Parked until a human sets creds up.
